title: styphelia rectiloba
intro: Styphelia rectiloba, also known as the Tasmanian button-sheoak or red button sheoak, is a species of flowering plant in the family Epacridaceae. It is a shrub native to the mountainous regions of Tasmania and southeastern Australia, valued for its medicinal properties and various health benefits. The plant has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Styphelia rectiloba is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety in modern medical practice.
